label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the given options has NOT been discussed in the passage. I need to go through each option and check if the passage talks about it. First, let me recap the passage. The main points are about a study comparing men and women's IQs and academic performance. It mentions that men are overrepresented both in the top 2% and bottom 2% of IQ scores, while women are more in the middle. The aggregate scores are similar. There's a psychologist, Timothy Bates, who suggests that societal expectations might play a role—men are expected to be high achievers, while women are restricted to home care. The passage also connects this to university degrees, where men get more first-class or third-class degrees, while women get more seconds. It says that the increase in coursework (which favors steady performance) might explain why women's scores are improving. Now, looking at the options: (A) Why are men smarter than women? The passage says that men are smarter in the top percentiles but also more in the bottom. The study found that men are in both extremes,
